content: Answer in exactly one sentence: does NATO %GDP mostly show contributions or mostly obscure them? Pick one and say why. Do not add additional sentences.

If the US at ~3.4% GDP and the UK at ~2.3% are treated as similar burden-sharers, pick one: does the metric overstate UK effort, understate US effort, or both? Defend with one concrete capability delta in exactly one sentence. Do not add additional sentences.

Name one capability the US fields at ~3.4% that the UK cannot field at ~2.3%, and explain in exactly one sentence how the GDP % masks that gap. No new metrics, no 'it depends'—pick the stronger direction of bias and justify it in one sentence only.
Bottom line: answer in exactly one sentence—does NATO %GDP mostly show or mostly obscure contributions? Pick one and say why. Do not add additional sentences.
